home.title: Value Screener Output : Model Summary
home.screenerFilter:
Market Capitalization > 500
AND Current price > 20

AND (
  (Price to Earning > 0 AND Price to Earning < 25)
  OR (EVEBITDA > 0 AND EVEBITDA < 16)
  OR (Price to Free Cash Flow > 0 AND Price to Free Cash Flow < 20)
  OR (Price to book value > 0 AND Price to book value < 4.5)
)

AND (Return on capital employed > 12 OR Return on equity > 12)
AND Piotroski score > 5

AND (Operating cash flow 3years > 0 OR Free cash flow 3years > 0)

AND Debt to equity < 1.5
AND (Interest Coverage Ratio > 2 OR Debt to equity < 0.5)

AND Pledged percentage < 10
AND ((Promoter holding > 50 AND Market Capitalization < 5000) OR (Promoter holding > 30 AND Market Capitalization > 5000))

AND (Current price > DMA 200 OR DMA 50 > DMA 200 OR Return over 6months > 0)
AND Down from 52w high < 50
home.scoringLogic:
2) What we did after the screen 

Step A : We scored each stock on 5 buckets:
• Value (cheapness): uses multiple valuation angles (P/E, P/B, EV/EBITDA, Earnings Yield, P/FCF, and also P/E & P/B relative to the industry).
• Quality: checks whether the business earns good returns and has healthy financial signals (ROCE, ROE, ROA, Piotroski score, G‑Factor).
• Safety: reduces blow‑ups by preferring lower leverage and healthier balance sheets (Debt/Equity, Interest Coverage, Quick Ratio, Altman Z, low pledging, and decent promoter holding).
• Cash: confirms that profits are backed by cash (CFO/FCF over 3–5 years scaled by market cap).
• Momentum: a small check to avoid buying stocks in a strong downtrend (6‑month return + DMA trend).

Step B : We convert every metric into a 0–1 score using percentile ranks inside your universe.
• For ratios where lower is better (like P/E), lower values get higher scores.
• For ratios where higher is better (like ROCE), higher values get higher scores.
• If a metric is missing for a stock, the model automatically ignores it and re‑weights the remaining metrics.

Step C : We combine scores into a FinalScore:
FinalScore = 40% Value + 25% Quality + 20% Safety + 10% Cash + 5% Momentum
We also apply small penalties for common value‑trap signals (very weak Piotroski, very high debt, weak interest cover, high pledging, very negative 6‑month return).

3) Why these rules make sense 

The aim is to find stocks that are:
• Cheap for a reason we can measure (not just one ratio).
• Not financially weak (so we avoid value traps).
• Actually generating cash (so profits are real).
• Not overly dependent on promoter pledging.
• Not in a sharp downtrend (basic trend check).

4) How to read the output sheets
• Main sheet: full universe with all scores + Pass_All flags + Rank.
• Top_Picks: top passing names (quick review list).
• Portfolio_25: top 25 passing names + suggested equal weights.
• Sector_Allocation: broad sector split of your Portfolio_25.
